REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT THOMAS LUCAS, TRUSTEE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED WITH 25 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. ALL OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS ARISE OUT OF INTERNAL TRUST MATTERS, INTERPRETATION AND VALIDITY OF TRUST DOCUMENTS, AND COLLATERAL ATTACK ON PRIOR PROBATE COURT ORDERS. ADDITIONALLY, IT APPEARS THAT MS. BENNETT HAS NO STANDING TO BRING THESE CLAIMS IN A CIVIL ACTION. PARTIES STIPULATE THAT MS. REDD WILL ACCEPT SERVICE OF THE ORDER AND WILL PREPARE THE AMENDED COMPLAINT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II; CLERK: J. MURPHY; REPORTED: SUZANNE ANDRADE, (415)597-5600, CSR#10682. (501)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-02-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Deniel A. Conrad, (415)359-0900 for Defendant/Moving party Thomas Lucas.
No other appearances.

No argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT THOMAS LUCAS, TRUSTEE DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Order signed in Open Court. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-02-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT THOMAS LUCAS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT (CCP 430.10).

To be heard in Department 501 at 9:30 am on the same date. All matters concerning real property are heard in Department 501.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Christine Jordan, CSR#12262, cjordan@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/RBU)

Defendant Thomas Lucas, Trustee'S Demurrer To Complaint
Off Calendar May-02-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Elisabeth Hanowsky, (415)359-0900 for Defendant Thomas Lucas.
LaToya Redd, (619)259-5559 appearing in Limited Scope for Plaintiff Christine Bennett.
Suzanne Andrade, (415)597-5600, CSR#10682.

Argued. The Court rules as follows: Defendant Thomas Lucas, Trustee's Demurrer To Complaint Sustained with 25 days leave to amend. All of Plaintiff's claims arise out of internal Trust matters, interpretation and validity of Trust documents, and collateral attack on prior Probate Court orders. Additionally, it appears that Ms. Bennett has no standing to bring these claims in a civil action. Parties stipulate that Ms. Redd will accept service of the order and will prepare the amended complaint. Order signed in open Court. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: J. Murphy; Reported: Suzanne Andrade, (415)597-5600, CSR#10682. (501)
